title: Durable water resistant coatings
abstract: Polymers comprising fluorinated side chains that can be used for water resistance coatings on substrates and are deposited by initiated chemical vapor deposition are generally described.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An article, comprising: a crosslinked polymer matrix comprising pendant groups, wherein the pendant groups comprise a C 1 -C7 fluoroalkyl group, and wherein the crosslinked polymer matrix has a water contact angle of greater than 90Â°. 2. The article of claim 1, wherein the C 1 -C7 fluoroalkyl group is not a C 6 fluoroalkyl group. 3. The article of claim 1, wherein the C 1 -C7 fluoroalkyl group is a C 7 fluoroalkyl group. 4. The article of claim 1, wherein the pendant groups comprise a C 1 -C5 fluoroalkyl group. 5. The article of any preceding claim, wherein the pendant groups comprise a perfluorinated group. 6. The article of any preceding claim, wherein the pendant groups comprise a spacer group. 7. The article of claim 6, wherein the spacer group comprises an ester group. 8. The article of any one of claims 6-7, wherein the spacer group comprises a methylene group. 9. The article of any one of claims 6-8, wherein the spacer group comprises a -C(0)-0- CH 2 - group. 10. The article of any one of claims 6-9, wherein the spacer group has a length of less than or equal to 6 angstroms. 11. The article of any one of claims 6-10, wherein the fluoroalkyl group is attached to a backbone of the crosslinked polymer matrix via the spacer group. 12. The article of any preceding claim, wherein the crosslinked polymer matrix is crosslinked with divinylbenzene. 13. The article of any preceding claim, wherein the crosslinked polymer matrix has a water contact angle hysteresis of less than or equal to about 30Â°. 14. The article of any preceding claim, wherein the crosslinked polymer matrix is positioned on a substrate, optionally wherein the crosslinked polymer matrix is deposited onto the substrate by initiated chemical vapor deposition. 15. The article of claim 14, wherein the crosslinked polymer matrix is covalently bound to the substrate. 16. The article of any one of claims 14-15, wherein the substrate is a fabric. 17. The article of any one of claims 14-15, wherein the substrate is paper. 18. The article of any preceding claim, wherein the crosslinked polymer matrix has a thickness of at least 5 nm. 19. A method, comprising: vaporizing a first monomer and a second monomer comprising a C1-C7 fluoroalkyl group in the presence of a free-radical initiator; depositing the first monomer and the second monomer on a substrate; and polymerizing the first monomer with the second monomer to form a crosslinked polymer matrix on the substrate; wherein the crosslinked polymer matrix comprises between 30 wt.% and 60 wt.% of the first monomer. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the substrate is sandblasted prior to deposition of the crosslinked polymer matrix. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the sandblasted substrate comprises microscale and/or nanoscale feature. 22. The method of any preceding claim, wherein the first monomer is divinylbenzene.
